prompt: Construct a ForgeOps Manufacturing & Workforce Hub supporting plant inbound receiving, production planning, and workforce onboarding in a single offline-ready site. On the Vue.js web interface, users sign in with a local username and password and land on role-based workspaces for Administrators, Materials/Receiving Clerks, Production Planners, HR Recruiters, Interviewers, and Candidates. Receiving Clerks schedule dock appointments in 30-minute windows, record PO receipt documents, capture receiving inspections, and follow guided putaway recommendations that validate bin capacity and mixed-storage rules (default: allow mixed storage only for the same SKU and lot). Discrepancies such as over/short or damaged items must be categorized and resolved with a disposition note before the receipt can be closed, preserving inbound traceability from PO line to lot/batch and storage location. Production Planners create a 12-week master production schedule, run material requirements planning, break work into work orders, and log production, rework, and downtime reasons with auditable plan adjustments that require a reason code and supervisor approval. HR workflows allow candidates to enter structured applicant information, complete configurable application forms, and upload attachments (PDF/JPG/PNG up to 20 MB each) that are auto-classified and checked for completeness; suspected duplicates are flagged when name plus date of birth plus last four digits of SSN match an existing record. A notification center provides in-app milestone subscriptions for ticket updates, review outcomes, adoption follow-ups, and receipt acknowledgments, with templates, configurable frequency (immediate, hourly digest, or once daily at 6:00 PM), and Do Not Disturb hours (9:00 PM–7:00 AM). Fast full-text search lets users find applicants, receipts, work orders, and notes by title/body/tags with synonyms and typo tolerance, plus filters by date range, source, topic, and entity. The backend uses Koa to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js client over a local network, keeping the system fully functional without internet access. MySQL is used for persistence of users, organizations/departments, applications and attachments, inbound documents, inventory locations, production plans and work orders, notification templates, and audit logs. Authorization is enforced with RBAC plus attribute-based rules (for example, interviewers can view only assigned candidates; receiving clerks can post receipts only for their site), and every create/update/approve action writes an immutable audit record with timestamp, actor, and before/after values. Passwords must be at least 12 characters, stored as salted hashes; after 5 failed attempts the account is locked for 15 minutes, and sessions idle out after 30 minutes. Sensitive fields (SSN last4, DOB) are encrypted at rest and masked in the UI unless the user has an explicit “Sensitive Data View” permission. The messaging subsystem supports pluggable channels, but in offline mode only in-app delivery is active; email/SMS/enterprise IM “connectors” generate exportable message files and a retry queue for later manual dispatch, ensuring no dependency on third-party services while still tracking delivery status and failed-send retries. A rules engine supports versioned scoring for internal training and qualification “grades,” calculating weighted final scores (default 40% coursework, 20% midterm, 40% final), converting to a 4.0 GPA scale, applying credit hours, and handling makeup/retake policies (default: highest score wins), with backtracking recalculation when a rule version changes.
